text.title: Error Handling Implementation
text.summary: FastAPI global exception handlers and error code constants following RFC 7807.

rule.statement: Define error codes as string constants grouped by 4xx vs 5xx; never use raw strings inline.
rule.rationale: Centralized constants prevent typos and make all error codes searchable in one place.

example.code:
class ErrorCode: # Client Errors (4xx) VALIDATION_ERROR = "validation_error" INVALID_FORMAT = "invalid_format" MISSING_FIELD = "missing_field" UNAUTHORIZED = "unauthorized" FORBIDDEN = "forbidden" NOT_FOUND = "not_found" CONFLICT = "conflict" RATE_LIMITED = "rate_limited"
# Server Errors (5xx) INTERNAL_ERROR = "internal_error" SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE = "service_unavailable" TIMEOUT = "timeout"

section.title: FastAPI Global Handlers

rule.statement: Register a global exception handler for ValidationError and a catch-all handler for Exception; log the catch-all server-side before responding.
rule.rationale: Without a catch-all, unhandled exceptions return raw 500 HTML from the framework, not a structured error body.

section.reference: FastAPI global exception handler wiring ProblemDetail

rule.statement: Error messages must be actionable: name the field, state the constraint, and describe the fix.
rule.rationale: "Invalid input" tells the client nothing; "Email must be in format user@domain.com" tells them exactly what to change.

example.note: No field name, no constraint, no action.
